subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Multiverse Hypothesis
description: A quick win: understand The Multiverse Hypothesis.

hook.text: What if our universe is just one of many? The multiverse hypothesis says countless universes may exist.

buildup.text: Eternal inflation suggests new universes bubble off constantly, each with different physical laws and constants.

discovery.text: String theory proposes 10⁵⁰⁰ possible configurations of physics. Each could describe a different universe.

twist.text: If true, the multiverse explains why our universe seems fine-tuned for life—we just happen to be in a lucky one.

climax.text: Critics argue the multiverse is untestable and therefore not real science. The debate divides physicists sharply.

punchline.text: Infinite universes, infinite laws. Inspiring idea—but possibly untestable.

quiz.question: What is the main criticism of the multiverse hypothesis?
options: It may be fundamentally untestable and therefore not scientific
It contradicts the theory of general relativity
It requires the existence of dark matter, which hasn't been found
